channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Getting real about AI strategy and deployment
item.description: Federal experts share real-world lessons on deploying AI across aging infrastructure, slow funding cycles, and workforce gaps. The post Getting real about AI strategy and deployment first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla’s ‘Robotaxi’ service is no Waymo
item.description: Greetings, salutations, and my thanks—as always—for reading Fast Company’s Plugged In. Me: “Are you here just to monitor for safety?” Guy sitting in the driver’s seat of the Tesla Model Y I’m riding in: “I can’t specify.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Google researchers say Oracle EBR hackers have hit dozens of organizations
item.description: The attacks started a lot earlier than previously thought, and bear the hallmarks of multiple threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Thinking of buying a new PC? You might want to move soon, as a perfect storm looks to be brewing for component price hikes
item.description: China's introducing new export controls on rare-earth elements - and that's bad news for the price of a raft of PC components.
item.dc:creator: Darren Allan, TechRadar

item.title: AI first: The EU’s new AI strategy
item.description:
EU Commission President von der Leyen called for an AI-first mindset at the presentation of the Apply AI and AI in Science Strategy.
miss.cabul – Shutterstock.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google and Figma partner in bid to remake real-time design with Gemini 2.5
item.description: Creativity has always been governed by time—not just how long it takes to bring an idea to life, but how long a creator can stay “in flow.” Every designer knows the frustration of an idea hanging in digital limbo. But those pauses, once accepted as inevitable, are now starting to vanish.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: Dominion Voting Systems sold and renamed as Liberty Vote
item.description: Dominion Voting Systems, the voting technology company that was at the center of controversy and false stolen-election claims after President Trump’s 2020 loss, has been sold and will be renamed as Liberty Vote, its new ownership announced on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Tesla’s ‘full self-driving’ faces another federal safety probe
item.description: US auto safety regulators are investigating Tesla Inc. over incidents in which its vehicles drove through red lights and violated other traffic laws while using the company's partial-automation software.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Gemini Enterprise is Google’s new ‘front door’ for agentic AI access at work
item.description: Google has introduced a new AI assistant to serve as a platform so users can access and coordinate agents that automate work tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: She helped North Korea infiltrate American tech companies
item.description: From her Arizona living room, Christina Chapman ran a covert hub that helped North Korean operatives infiltrate U.S. firms, netting $17 million in stolen salaries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Executive Survey: Amplifying federal service delivery
item.description: In partnership with Maximus, Federal News Network surveyed government executives and managers to understand how agencies are meeting these challenges head-on. The post Executive Survey: Amplifying federal service delivery first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: UC Santa Barbara develops new soft robotic system for emergency intubation procedures
item.description: UC Santa Barbara researchers developed a soft robotic intubation system that achieved 100% success rates for experts and 96% for paramedics with minimal training.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cisco seriously amps-up Silicon One chip, router for AI data center connectivity
item.description: Cisco has unwrapped a high-end, 51.2 Tbps router and chip that it says will go a long way toward supporting the distributed AI workloads of today and in the future.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump’s coal bailout won’t solve the data center power crunch
item.description: The Trump administration is spending more than half a billion dollars to help prop up the dying coal industry. It’s also weakening pollution regulations and opening up more federal land to coal mining.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: From pilot to production: How large enterprises can scale XR projects
item.description: Broad adoption of augmented and virtual reality (AR and VR) technologies remains at an early stage, but many businesses are already seeing value in the systems they’ve rolled out: immersive training for frontline employees, for example, or engineers who can collaborate on design using 3D models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Key crypto agency on uncertain footing after White House pulls nominee
item.description: The White House’s decision to pull its nominee to lead the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has left the financial regulator in an increasingly uncertain position, with just one remaining commissioner in place to tackle President Trump’s ambitious crypto agenda.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How GitHub won software development
item.description: In the late 2000s, I was working at Borland, the formerly high-flying software development tools company. My boss and I would regularly sit around tossing out ideas for new products. The Internet was really hitting its stride, and we saw numerous opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nobel Prize in chemistry goes to discovery that could trap C02 and bring water to deserts
item.description: Three scientists have won the Nobel Prize in chemistry for their development of new molecular structures that can trap vast quantities of gas inside.
item.dc:creator: Kostya Manenkov And Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sanders: AI may take 100 million jobs in the next 10 years
item.description: Bernie Sanders says in a new ChatGPT-assisted report released Monday that AI and automation could eliminate nearly 100 million jobs over the next decade. Titled “Big Tech Oligarchs’ War Against Workers: AI and Automation Could Destroy Nearly 100 Million U.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Snapchat users are going to have to pay to keep their memories
item.description: First came the burning of the Library of Alexandria. Then came the news that Snapchat is constructing a paywall around Memories. The company announced last week that it is capping users’ free Memories storage at 5 gigabytes (GB).
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: JetBrains Amper build tool adds Compose Hot Reload
item.description: JetBrains has released Amper 0.8.0, an update to the build tool for Java and Kotlin that adds support for Compose Hot Reload. Amper 0.8.0 also adds support for custom Kotlin compiler versions and for JavaScript and WebAssembly (Wasm) targets.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tesla offers cheaper versions of 2 electric vehicles in bid to win back market share in tough year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tesla rolled out new, cheaper versions of two of its electric car models on Tuesday in hopes the offerings will help revive flagging sales but investors dumped its stock anyway.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kentucky lawsuit says Roblox fails to protect children on its popular online gaming platform
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky's attorney general claimed Tuesday that the online gaming platform Roblox has become a “playground for predators” as he announced a lawsuit accusing the company of lax child safety measures.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: IBM integrates Anthropic Claude into AI IDE and other tools
item.description: IBM and Anthropic have partnered in an effort to accelerate the development of enterprise AI applications, by integrating Anthropic’s Claude large language models (LLMs) into IBM software products, beginning with IBM’s new AI-leveraging IDE (integrated development environment).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DevRev’s AI agent hangout targets worker productivity, data integration
item.description: Imagine a city hub where a multitude of AI agents can gather, interact, and converse. Office workers are more like sentries — seeing and interacting with the information generated by agents at the hub.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI and chipmaker AMD sign chip supply partnership for AI infrastructure
item.description: Semiconductor maker AMD will supply its chips to artificial intelligence company OpenAI as part of an agreement to team up on building AI infrastructure, the companies said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
